signals.headline: Cavaliers lead Raptors 2-0 after Game 2 win (115-105)** | YES | HIGH
signals.raw:
Cavaliers lead Raptors 2-0 after Game 2 win (115-105)** | YES | HIGH  
Cleveland Cavaliers defeated Toronto Raptors in Game 2 of Eastern Conference first round, securing a 2-0 series lead with dominant play including three players scoring 25+ points and stifling defense on Brandon Ingram (7 points on 3-15 shooting).[3] This advances their path toward potential semifinals matchup vs. Detroit Piston
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: Lakers clinched top-6 West seed & playoffs (no lower than #6, trending #3)** | YES | HIGH
signals.raw:
Lakers clinched top-6 West seed & playoffs (no lower than #6, trending #3)** | YES | HIGH  
Lakers won 127-113 over Cavaliers on March 31, 2026, locking playoff spot with 50 regular-season wins and strong post-All-Star surge (16-18 overall, 15-2 in March).[2] Recent Game 1 win over Rockets highlighted veteran performances amid playoff context.[1]
